description_long: This object is a biplane-style airplane embedded in a rough, irregular slab of terrain or debris. The airplane features two stacked wings with a straight, rectangular planform and visible struts connecting the upper and lower wings. The fuselage is slender and elongated, with a distinct cross-shaped profile when viewed from above. The color scheme includes a white and red pattern on the wings and fuselage, contrasting with the gray, rocky surface surrounding the airplane. The tail section is visible, with a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from the rear. The airplane appears partially sunken or fused into the surrounding material, giving it a distressed or crash-landed appearance.
description: A biplane-style airplane with white and red markings is embedded in a rough, gray slab of terrain, with visible wings, fuselage, and tail surfaces.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full biplane structure, white and red wing markings, and the airplane's integration into the rough terrain.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the airplane's embedding in the terrain and the cross-section of the wings and fuselage.
description_view_2: Angled rear-side view emphasizing the sunken nature of the airplane and the rough, uneven surface of the surrounding material.
description_view_3: Frontal view displaying the biplane's nose, stacked wings, and the clear cross-shaped profile against the gray terrain.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing biplane configuration with straight, rectangular wings and visible interplane struts; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit located at the front of the fuselage, likely single-seat.
